articles.summary: Gaza death toll rises to 73,035 as Israeli attacks continue The death toll from Israel's genocide in Gaza has risen to 73,035, with 173,368 Palestinians wounded since October 2023, according to medical sources cited by the Palestinian news agency Wafa. The figures also indicate that 1,005 Palestinians have been killed since the US-brokered ceasefire came into effect in October, highlighting the continued deadly toll of Israeli attacks despite the agreement. articles.title: Israeli attacks have killed over 4,100 people in Lebanon
articles.summary: Israeli attacks have killed over 4,100 people in Lebanon Lebanon's Ministry of Public Health says at least 4,175 people have been killed in Israeli attacks since 2 March, according to figures released by the ministry's Health Emergency Operations Center. The ministry added that 12,164 people have been wounded over the same period, underscoring the heavy human toll of Israel's military campaign in Lebanon. The latest figures come as Israeli strikes continue across southern Lebanon despite ongoing diplomatic efforts aimed at reducing regional tensions following the US-Iran agreement.

articles.title: Ship traffic through Strait of Hormuz remains below pre-war levels
articles.summary: Ship traffic through Strait of Hormuz remains below pre-war levels Commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz remains well below pre-war levels, according to data from analytics firm Kpler, despite vessels gradually returning to the strategic waterway. Kpler recorded 71 confirmed transits over the weekend, including a peak of 35 crossings on Saturday. Before the US-Israeli war on Iran, between 100 and 130 vessels passed through the strait each day.
